Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Shell Tricks That Make Life Easier (and Save Your Sanity) 25 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Two studies in compiler optimisations 66 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. More precise elevation data for GraphHopper routing engine 21 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. Power consumption of Game Boy flash cartridges (2021) 28 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. Quantization from the Ground Up 242 upvotes, 46 comments
  6. Data is everywhere. The government is buying it without a warrant 22 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. My astrophotography in the movie Project Hail Mary 836 upvotes, 197 comments
  8. The EU still wants to scan your private messages and photos 1024 upvotes, 267 comments
  9. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 569 upvotes, 172 comments
  10. FreeCAD v1.1 246 upvotes, 75 comments
  11. Show HN: A plain-text cognitive architecture for Claude Code 86 upvotes, 25 comments
  12. My DIY FPGA board can run Quake II 130 upvotes, 43 comments
  13. Rendering complex scripts in terminal and OSC 66 30 upvotes, 6 comments
  14. False claims in a widely-cited paper 253 upvotes, 97 comments
  15. Thoughts on slowing the fuck down 812 upvotes, 376 comments
  16. Earthquake scientists reveal how overplowing weakens soil at experimental farm 149 upvotes, 61 comments
  17. The truth that haunts the Ramones: 'They sold more T-shirts than records' 85 upvotes, 35 comments
  18. Apple randomly closes bug reports unless you "verify" the bug remains unfixed 372 upvotes, 214 comments
  19. 90% of Claude-linked output going to GitHub repos w <2 stars 259 upvotes, 153 comments
  20. Woman who never stopped updating her lost dog's chip reunites with him after 11y 167 upvotes, 94 comments
  21. ARC-AGI-3 370 upvotes, 246 comments
  22. Sodium-ion EV battery breakthrough delivers 11-min charging and 450 km range 174 upvotes, 119 comments
  23. Supreme Court Sides with Cox in Copyright Fight over Pirated Music 334 upvotes, 258 comments
  24. Show HN: Optio – Orchestrate AI coding agents in K8s to go from ticket to PR 46 upvotes, 28 comments
  25. "Disregard That" Attacks 71 upvotes, 50 comments
  26. Show HN: Robust LLM Extractor for Websites in TypeScript 32 upvotes, 19 comments
  27. Jury finds Meta liable in case over child sexual exploitation on its platforms 375 upvotes, 470 comments
  28. Do Architects Still Need to Draw? (2020) 14 upvotes, 11 comments
  29. What Came After the 486? 6 upvotes, 4 comments
  30. Ashby (YC W19) Is Hiring Engineers Who Make Product Decisions 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 4:00pm, 26 March 2026. Source here.
